subject: jee-main
title: JEE Main 2026 (Apr 2, Shift 1)

source._verified: 2026-06-12 HTTP 200 + page content checked (Aakash memory-based Answers & Solutions, 02-04-2026 Morning = Shift 1)

_provenance: Questions + answers transcribed from Aakash Institute's 'Memory Based Answers & Solutions' PDFs for JEE (Main) 2026 Phase-2, 02-04-2026 Morning (subject-wise: Maths/Physics/Chemistry), read eyes-on by Opus vision. Every question carries the institute-VERIFIED correct answer. This is a MEMORY-BASED reconstruction by a coaching institute (not the official NTA paper), and is PARTIAL: questions Aakash could not recall are omitted, and a few items whose options are drawn chemical/physics structures or whose printed answer was internally inconsistent were excluded — nothing invented. 51 questions total (Maths 16, Physics 15, Chemistry 20). Figure-dependent questions carry a [Figure: ...] text description (no image file).

contract._comment: Memory-based coaching reconstruction; sections grouped by subject (A = MCQ, B = numeric). Marks 4 each. totalQuestions reflects the verified subset actually built, contiguously numbered 1..N.

[Figure: Two arrangements of wires, each carrying current I, each with a semi-circular part of radius R at the centre C; find the ratio of magnetic field at the centre of the semi-circular part of the two arrangements.]
Consider two arrangements of wires; find the ratio of magnetic field at the centre of the semi-circular part.
In an isobaric reversible process on a diatomic gas, the ratio of ΔQ : ΔU : ΔW shall be
In circular motion, angular position θ and time t are related as θ = 5t⁴/4 − t³/3. Then the angular acceleration at t = 10 s is
For an ideal gas having Cp = 3R and Cv = 2R, find the work done by one mole of the gas in an adiabatic expansion when pressure reduces from 8 bar to 1 bar. (Initial temperature = 140 °C)
In a YDSE experiment the wavelength of light used is 620 nm and the separation between slits is 0.2 mm. Find the angular fringe width.
Photons of wavelength λ and 3λ are incident on a metal surface. If the stopping potentials for the ejected photoelectrons are 4V₀ and V₀ respectively, find the threshold wavelength.

[Figure: A single spherical refracting surface of radius of curvature R = 20 cm; an object of height h₀ = 2 mm is placed at 40 cm from the surface; medium on the two sides has refractive index μ₁ = 1 and μ₂ = 1.5.]
For the given spherical surface of radius of curvature R = 20 cm, an object of height h₀ = 2 mm is placed at a distance 40 cm from the surface. The media on the two sides of the spherical surface have refractive indices μ₁ = 1 and μ₂ = 1.5 respectively. Find the height of the image.
Find the change in surface energy if 512 small drops of radius r = 2 mm merge into a single large drop. (surface tension = S)

[Figure: An empty parallel-plate capacitor of capacitance C₀; one-fourth of its volume is filled with a dielectric slab of constant K = 5 (the slab has surface area A/2 and width d/2).]
One-fourth volume of an empty capacitor of capacitance C₀ is filled with a dielectric of constant K = 5. The surface area of the dielectric is A/2 and width d/2. If A ≫ d², then the new capacitance is

[Figure: Two hollow conducting spheres (radii 8 cm and 18 cm) separated by a large distance, connected by a conducting wire.]
Two hollow conducting spheres are separated by a large distance. They are connected by a conducting wire. If E₁ and E₂ are the magnitudes of the electric field near the surface of sphere (1) (radius 8 cm) and sphere (2) (radius 18 cm) respectively, then find the ratio E₁/E₂.

[Figure: Two satellites revolve around two planets in circular orbits: planet of mass M with orbit radius r, and planet of mass 2M with orbit radius 2r.]
Two satellites are revolving around two planets in circular orbits as shown (mass M, radius r; and mass 2M, radius 2r). Find the ratio of their time periods T₁/T₂.

[Figure: An inductor coil (solenoid).]

questions.msAnswer: Correct numerical answer: 2. Mark correct only if the learner's value equals 2.

[Figure: A P–V diagram: gas goes from state A (P = 8 atm, V = 0.5 L) to state B (P = 1 atm, V = 4 L) along a curve (isothermal-like).]
Calculate the work in the following process during A to B (given Cv = 3R), with A at (8 atm, 0.5 L) and B at (1 atm, 4 L).
Match List-I (Molecules) with List-II (Test): (I) Ethanol, (II) Phenol, (III) Ethanoic Acid, (IV) Acetaldehyde ; (P) Neutral FeCl₃, (Q) Ceric ammonium nitrate, (R) Schiff reagent, (S) NaHCO₃.
Which of the following relations about first-order and zero-order reactions is correct?
options: (t100%)_first order = infinite time of t½ of first order
(t100%)_zero order = infinite time of t½ of zero order
questions.correct: (t100%)_first order = infinite time of t½ of first order
questions.msAnswer: (t100%)_first order = infinite time of t½ of first order

questions.prompt: Given two statements — Statement I: First I.E. order is Na > Mg > Al. Statement II: 3rd I.E. order is Mg > Al > Na. Choose the correct option.
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
questions.correct: Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
questions.msAnswer: Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect

questions.prompt: For a first-order reaction the rate constants at 27 °C and t °C are 1.5 × 10³ and 4.5 × 10³ respectively. If the activation energy of the reaction is 60 kJ, then find the temperature t in °C. (R = 8.3 J mol⁻¹ K⁻¹)
questions.msAnswer: Correct numerical answer: 41. Mark correct only if the learner's value equals 41 (tolerance ±1).

questions.prompt: Calculate the number of isomers of C₅H₁₀ which do NOT decolorise cold alkaline KMnO₄ solution.
questions.msAnswer: Correct numerical answer: 7. Mark correct only if the learner's value equals 7.

questions.prompt: 8 g of [CrClₓ(H₂O)_y]Cl_(3−x)·H₂O_(6−y) reacts with AgNO₃ to form 8.61 g of AgCl. The value of (x × y) is

_coverageNote: All 51 questions are answer-keyed (MCQ via deterministic grader; numeric via exact-value rubric) and node-mapped. Contiguous 1..51.
